title: Publication

content:
Research Directions Our research focuses on understanding, predicting, and assessing hydroclimate extremes in a warming climate. We combine climate observations, model simulations, hydrological modelling, machine learning, and probabilistic risk analysis to study floods, droughts, heatwaves, and compound water-related hazards.
Compound Events We are interested in how and why compound events, including concurrent droughts and heatwaves as well as dry-wet abrupt alternation, are changing and affecting our society in a warming climate.
Climate Extremes and Inequality Climate extremes do not affect all communities equally. We study how floods, droughts, heatwaves, and abrupt hydroclimatic shifts interact with socioeconomic vulnerability, exposure, and adaptation capacity, with the goal of understanding who is most affected by emerging climate risks and why.

title: People

content:
About Me Hi, I am currently an assistant professor of hydroclimatology in the School of Geography and Planning at Sun Yat-sen University (SYSU). I obtained my PhD in hydrology and climate science from The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU) in 2022, under the supervision of Prof. Shuo Wang. Before joining SYSU, I was a postdoctoral researcher in the Hydro-Climate Extremes Research Group led by Prof. Louise Slater at the University of Oxford.
My research focuses on extreme weather and climate events, machine learning-based flood prediction, climate change impacts, multivariate statistics, including copulas and pair-copula constructions, and hydrological modelling. I use machine learning methods and climate model outputs to improve global flood prediction and projection. I also develop and apply multivariate probabilistic approaches to assess the characteristics, hazards, and risks of hydroclimate extremes under climate change.
